properties.titulo_evento: Cost of Iran war rises to $37.5 billion, Pentagon requests $67.1 billion more
properties.descricao: The Iran war has cost the DoD $37.5 billion to date, Hegseth told senators Tuesday, as he requested $67.1 billion more in emergency funding.

properties.titulo_evento: New British prime minister gains early defense win with Canada fighter jet tie-up
properties.descricao: Canada has joined the GCAP fighter program as an observer, marking the first expansion beyond the founding members Britain, Italy and Japan.

properties.titulo_evento: Iran warns Bulgaria against aiding US military operations
properties.descricao: Iran’s foreign ministry spokesman warned Bulgaria on Tuesday against allowing the U.S. to use its territory for military operations against Iran.

properties.titulo_evento: Germany to study French cooperation offer on SAMP/T air-defense system
properties.descricao: German government leaders will study an offer by French President Emmanuel Macron to cooperate on the French-Italian SAMP/T air defense system.

properties.titulo_evento: Russia may use captured Ukrainian drones against Poland and Baltics, Warsaw warns
properties.descricao: Russia has captured a large number of Ukrainian drones and may be planning to use them against NATO or to stage a false flag attack, Polish officials said.

properties.titulo_evento: Doodle Labs eyes US, European production sites as demand soars for jam-resistant drones
properties.descricao: Doodle Labs hopes to turn its success in equipping jam-hardened drones in Ukraine into additional production sites in Europe and he United States.

properties.titulo_evento: Israel’s IAI unveils ‘Hypnosis’ system for confusing enemy drone swarms
properties.descricao: The system, called Hypnosis, consists of an array of mobile “stations” around a defended area that send out jamming signals against drone swarms.

properties.titulo_evento: Iran war live: US launches new attacks; Hegseth says war has cost $37.5bn
properties.descricao: Trump threatens to bomb a suspected Iranian nuclear site called Pickaxe Mountain 'very heavily'.

properties.titulo_evento: US attacks Iran for 11th consecutive night
properties.descricao: Explosions reported in northwestern Iran's Tabriz, capital Tehran and across southern Iran.

properties.titulo_evento: Lebanese army deploys in ‘pilot zones’ under US-backed plan
properties.descricao: The Lebanese army has begun deploying in three southern towns as part of a US-backed framework agreement with Israel.

properties.titulo_evento: Trump administration attacks US left, alleging Cuba ties: Key takeaways
properties.descricao: US report says the beleaguered government in Havana is the 'connective tissue' of a global 'anti-American coalition'.

properties.titulo_evento: Zelenskyy replaces Ukraine military chief after protests
properties.descricao: Replaced military chief Syrskii clashed with former Defence Minister Fedorov, whose removal last week led to protests.

properties.titulo_evento: Drone shows scorched landscape after Spain wildfire
properties.descricao: A drone has revealed what remains of this forest after a wildfire burned through over 30,000 hectares of it.

properties.titulo_evento: US defence chief Hegseth puts Iran war cost at $37.5bn so far
properties.descricao: Latest cost comes as Hegseth and Joint Chiefs Chairman Caine seek a $1.5 trillion budget, including $70bn for Iran war.

properties.titulo_evento: Iran holds funerals for children killed in Minab school strike
properties.descricao: Iran has held funerals for 32 children killed in a US strike on a school in Minab, Iran on February 28.

properties.titulo_evento: Daytime Russian strike kills three in Zaporizhzhia
properties.descricao: A Russian guided aerial bomb attack on Zaporizhzhia, Ukraine, killed at least three people and injured 13 others.

properties.titulo_evento: Carney says he and Trump have agreed to ‘intensify’ trade negotiations
properties.descricao: Relations between Canada and its largest trading partner have been tested by Trump's repeated jibes, threats and tariffs

properties.titulo_evento: Can mediators broker a truce between the US and Iran?
properties.descricao: With every new round of attacks raising political cost of compromise, analysts warn the window for peace may be closing.

properties.titulo_evento: Carney looking at 'all options' as Trump announces 50% tariffs on Canada
properties.descricao: The duties mark a major escalation in trade tensions between the North American neighbours.

properties.titulo_evento: US launches fresh strikes on Iran, as Trump warns of retaliation for deaths of soldiers
properties.descricao: Iran says it has hit two ships in the Strait of Hormuz as well as targets in Bahrain and Jordan.

properties.titulo_evento: Israeli strikes in Gaza kill 12, including family of six, medics say
properties.descricao: Firas al-Masri, his wife and their four children died in one attack, which the Israeli military says targeted a Hamas operative.

properties.titulo_evento: Aliyev says former Russian and German officials were in Baku for possible meeting over Ukraine
properties.descricao: Azerbaijan’s president revealed that former Russian and German officials were in Baku at the same time earlier in July, but that Azerbaijan’s authorities were neither notified nor aware of the meeting, which might have involved discussions over Ukrai...

properties.titulo_evento: Aliyev visits Berlin to deepen energy and trade ties with Germany
properties.descricao: Azerbaijan and Germany signed a strategic partnership declaration covering energy, transport and trade, with Chancellor Merz welcoming Azerbaijan's gas deliveries, expected to reach 2 billion cubic metres annually by next year, as a further step away...

properties.titulo_evento: Iran vows to bomb own land as fears grow over Kuwait incursion
properties.descricao: Iranian MPs called for capturing US soldiers and launching ground attacks on Kuwait and Bahrain, as discussions grew over a possible ground operation by either side and US strikes entered their 10th consecutive day.

properties.titulo_evento: Lebanese army begins deployment to southern regions after Israel deal
properties.descricao: Beirut announced the beginning of its mobilisation to the southern Lebanese regions as President Aoun prepares for a high-stakes meeting with US President Donald Trump on Tuesday to conclude his four-day visit to Washington.

properties.titulo_evento: Iran's IRGC claims attack on Amazon's main data hub in Bahrain
properties.descricao: The IRGC claimed its cruise missiles destroyed Amazon's central data infrastructure in Bahrain and hit US air defence systems. Bahraini, US and Amazon officials have not responded.

properties.titulo_evento: Drone strike sparks major fire in Sumy, northeastern Ukraine
properties.descricao: A home improvement shopping centre in Sumy, Ukraine, catches fire after a reported Russian FPV drone strike as firefighters battle the blaze.

properties.titulo_evento: Tanker attacked in Strait of Hormuz as US strikes Iran for tenth consecutive night
properties.descricao: As fighting escalates between both sides, Iran's interior minister travelled to Pakistan, a key mediator in the conflict, for talks.

properties.titulo_evento: Yemen’s Houthis threaten Bab el-Mandeb closure in embargo on Saudi Arabia
properties.descricao: The Iran-backed Houthis declared a maritime embargo on Saudi Arabia after accusing the kingdom of attacking Sana'a airport and maintaining a years-long blockade, putting one of the world's busiest shipping lanes at risk.

properties.titulo_evento: IRGC pledges 'unforgettable lesson' for US as Iran signals return to talks
properties.descricao: The Revolutionary Guard told Ayatollah Khamenei it regarded his message as "a binding command," as Iran's foreign ministry said diplomacy remained open and mediators sought a 10-day ceasefire to revive the framework deal.

properties.titulo_evento: Hegseth says Iran war has cost $37.5 billion so far
properties.descricao: US Pentagon chief Pete Hegseth is also pushing for more funding for the war. It comes as the US military is striking Iran for the 11th consecutive night. DW has the latest.

properties.titulo_evento: Ukraine's Zelenskyy fires military chief after days of protests
properties.descricao: Mass protests in recent days in Kyiv and other Ukrainian cities had called for the removal of commander-in-chief Oleksandr Syrskyi. Volodymyr Zelenskyy has named Mykhailo Drapatyi as the army's new chief.

properties.titulo_evento: Jordan faces growing risk of becoming latest frontline state in US-Iran conflict
properties.descricao: As Iranian attacks intensify, Jordan is emerging as the latest frontline state in the widening Middle East conflict. Why is the Hashemite Kingdom a target for Tehran?

properties.titulo_evento: Fact check: Trump wasn't edited out of Spain World Cup photo
properties.descricao: After Spain won the FIFA World Cup, the Spanish Football Federation posted a photo of the trophy celebrations, raising questions about whether Donald Trump had been edited out. We took a closer look.

properties.titulo_evento: French lawmakers approve social media ban for children
properties.descricao: Lawmakers in France gave the green light to a social media ban that could come into effect by the start of the new school year in September, becoming the first EU country to enact such a ban.

properties.titulo_evento: China narrows AI gap, challenges US influence
properties.descricao: At the World Artificial Intelligence Conference in Shanghai, Beijing showcased advances in AI while promoting its vision of open-source development and global governance.

properties.titulo_evento: Is Germany investing in the right weapons?
properties.descricao: Germany wants to build up its army and pour hundreds of millions of euros into its defenses. But in a world where the battlefield is increasingly dominated by cheap drones, how many expensive tanks does the country need?

properties.titulo_evento: Russia says bus strike kills 5 in border region with Ukraine
properties.descricao: The strike comes as Ukraine launched more than hundreds of drones at Moscow after a Russian missile barrage on Kyiv, as President Zelenskyy sought to calm growing tensions over Ukraine’s military leadership.

properties.titulo_evento: Hegseth says Iran is weakened, but won’t give timeline for war’s end
properties.descricao: The administration is seeking tens of billions to cover the cost of the conflict, but Democrats are opposed to the idea.

properties.titulo_evento: Zelenskyy fires top army commander amid nationwide protests
properties.descricao: New army chief Mykhailo Drapatyi had spoken out against the Ukraine president's controversial decision to fire the country's defense minister last week.

properties.titulo_evento: Trump says midterms won’t impact his Iran war plan
properties.descricao: Republicans are increasingly anxious over the potential ramifications of the war amid rising gas prices and the death of three troops since fighting resumed.

properties.titulo_evento: Newmont Stock Suddenly Offers a Double-Sided Debit Trade on U.S.-Iran Tensions and Upcoming Earnings
Houthis Threaten Red Sea Blockade, Putting Oil Market at Greater Risk
properties.descricao: With the Strait of Hormuz effectively closed, the militant group’s threats in the Red Sea jeopardize Saudi Arabia’s main workaround for oil exports.

properties.titulo_evento: Oil Buyers Battered by the Iran War Energy Crisis Race to Build Buffers
properties.descricao: As rising fuel costs clobber their economies, the Philippines and other developing countries in Asia are pushing to build strategic petroleum reserves to cushion against future shocks.

properties.titulo_evento: Andy Burnham, New UK Prime Minister, Names John Healey as Top Finance Official
properties.descricao: As chancellor, John Healey, who quit as defense secretary in the former government, will fill one of the most consequential posts, overseeing fiscal and economic policy.
